name: France
description: France is a global hub of culture, fashion, and innovation. Home to world-class Grandes Écoles and public universities, France offers excellent education at remarkably affordable tuition rates.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: France is the only country in Europe to offer this benefit. All students are eligible on a case-to-case basis to apply for assistance and receive subsidy on cost of accommodation from 30% to 50% of the rent from the French Government.
tuitionFees.postgraduate: French classes are provided by many Government organizations for free. Enrolment fees are typically around €5 to €10. Duration remains 2 to 3 hours a day. It is part of the course curriculum for which exams are conducted and students are awarded a certificate for their French knowledge level.
tuitionFees.phd: €380–€400 at public universities; often funded through research contracts

scholarships.name: For French Government scholarship holder : attestation certifying the amount and duration of the scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Covering letter mentioning the purpose of the trip, duration, sponsors, motivation… Curriculum Vitae

requirements: University acceptance letter / Campus France approval
French health insurance or private coverage
Language proficiency (French B2 for French programs / IELTS for English)

workPermit.duringStudy: Up to 964 hours per year (approximately 20 hours per week)
workPermit.postStudy: APS Visa: 1 year to find work in France after graduation

languageRequirements.notes: French B2 required for French-taught programs; many English-taught programs available

topUniversities.name: Queen Mary University of London Institute in Paris

faqs.question: Is it easy to get a student visa for France?
faqs.answer: With proper documentation and financial proof, obtaining a student visa for France is straightforward. MVR Consultants can guide you through the entire process.

faqs.question: What are the tuition fees in France?
faqs.answer: Tuition fees vary by university and program. Contact our counselors for program-specific fee structures.

faqs.question: Can I work while studying?
faqs.answer: Most countries allow international students to work part-time during term time. Specific hours and conditions depend on the country's immigration rules.

faqs.question: Are there scholarships available for Indian students?
faqs.answer: Yes, many universities and governments offer merit-based and need-based scholarships for international students. Ask our counselors for scholarship matching.
